Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Ronda Calef's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted as to the proposed fourth cause of action for elder financial abuse and the proposed sixth cause of action for violation of the CLRA on specific conditions and denied as to the proposed fifth cause of action for violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. The proposed new claims for elder financial abuse and violation of the CLRA are arguably tenable. As conditions for allowing her to allege these new claims at this stage of the case Ms. Calef must: 1) within twenty days serve code-compliant verified responses to deemed-served discovery requiring her to state all facts in support of these two new claims, identify and provide contact information for all persons who know such facts and state what facts each of them know, and identify all documents that refer or relate to such facts and produce those documents to the extent they have not already been produced and 2) at the option of defendants, agree to either a) a four month continuance of the trial or b) defendants' filing a summary adjudication motion as to the two new claims on 40 days' notice. Defendants' counsel must apprise Ms. Calef's counsel which option defendants choose within five days. While Ms. Calef has been less than fully diligent in seeking to add these two new claims, California's liberal policy allowing amendments to pleadings and the absence of any prejudice to defendants as a result of the conditions placed on allowing these two new claims favors granting Ms. Calef leave to allege these two claims. Ms. Calef's proposed claim for violation of the Debt Collection Practices Act is improper as a matter of law because defendants are not "debt collectors" as defined in Civil Code 1788.2(c) and the underlying debt is not a derived from a "consumer credit transaction" as defined in Civil Code 1788.2(e). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
